hie_survivorship_logic: Most reliable source (death certificate > vital records > hospital discharge disposition > mortality file), immutable once verified, timestamp required, blocks identity merges with living patients

Name: Patient.death_date

hie_survivorship_logic: Most reliable source, medical record > government ID > self-report for clinical purposes
innovaccer_survivorship_logic: Ask Innovaccer what logic they use to arrive at SBR

hie_survivorship_logic: Most recent self-reported value, timestamp required, patient preference always wins

hie_survivorship_logic: Most recent self-reported value, timestamp required, patient preference always wins

hie_survivorship_logic: Detailed > high-level (Japanese > Asian), support multiple values, most recent self-report preferred
innovaccer_survivorship_logic: Ask Innovaccer what logic they use to arrive at SBR
data_quality_notes: 28 race values; granularity varies by source; Full structure includes ombCategory (5 high-level), detailed (granular), and text; PRIORITY: .extension(detailed) > .extension(ombCategory) when both present; US CORE REQUIREMENT: ombCategory is MANDATORY (1..1), detailed is optional (0..*); Logic: look for detailed first, fallback to ombCategory
fhir_data_type: Complex Extension: ombCategory (Coding 1..1 REQUIRED), detailed (Coding 0..*), text (string)
Name: Patient.race

hie_survivorship_logic: Most recent self-reported value, detailed > high-level (Mexican > Hispanic or Latino)
innovaccer_survivorship_logic: Ask Innovaccer what logic they use to arrive at SBR

fhir_data_type: Complex Extension: ombCategory (Coding 1..1 REQUIRED), detailed (Coding 0..*), text (string)
Name: Patient.ethnicity
